text:
Once upon an autumn day, in a quaint little village nestled among whispering pines, there lived a wise owl named Ottora. She knew every bird song and listened keenly to every squirrel’s tale.

One particular afternoon, Ottora felt unusually hungry. She fluttered down to the nut stash she’d placed by the hedge earlier. There were plenty!

As she picked grains, she thought about stories. Stories about brave rabbits. She imagined herself hopping across moors.

Then, she thunked upon an idea richer than any berry berry. Tales of a forest creature so gentle and wise. Why not combine hers?

“A hare named Silky!”

She fashioned her story. Silky loved acorns. So did Ottora. They’d climb together.

But then Ottora got creative. They’d build a house!

Ottora sculpted straw with feathers. So did Silkie. Together they decorated.

And when winter came?

“Oh, lots of snow!” said Ottora.
